recent_hooks.topic_thesis: Rectification of Names matters because a leader who cannot call failure failure makes ritual truth-telling a farce.
recent_hooks.angle: Do not summarize the news; diagnose the pattern underneath it.
recent_hooks.hook: When a leader calls a layoff a "right-sizing," a lost deal a "strategic reprioritization," or a failed product "a learning journey," they think they're protecting morale. They're actually destroying the one thing that makes truth-telling possible.
recent_hooks.summary:
When a leader calls a layoff a "right-sizing," a lost deal a "strategic reprioritization," or a failed product "a learning journey," they think they're protecting morale. They're actually destroying the one thing that makes truth-telling possible.

Confucius had a term for this:

recent_hooks.topic_thesis: Severability and reversibility make criticism cheaper and therefore more honest.
recent_hooks.angle: Start with a recurring problem and end with a usable move.
recent_hooks.hook: Most teams know what's wrong. They just can't afford to say it.
recent_hooks.summary:
Most teams know what's wrong. They just can't afford to say it.

The cost of criticism is almost never intellectual. It's social. People stay quiet because speaking up risks their status, their relationships, their safety. So errors persist—not because no one sees them, but becau
